member.name: Steve Barclay

addresses: MJS House, Wisbech Road, Westry, March, PE15 0BA

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Steve Barclay is the Conservative MP for North East Cambridgeshire , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescriptions: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the answer to Question 10108 on 24 June 2026, where data on NHS prescriptions issued to not ordinarily resident overseas visitors is held.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This data is not routinely collected.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many not ordinarily resident overseas visitors received NHS primary health care services in (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not held by the Department. General Practice Appointment Data records the number of appointments delivered to registered patients. It does not capture information on the migration status of those who received care.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund: Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how much funding through the Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund has been awarded to projects focused on Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products (ATMPs).
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed up to £520m for life sciences manufacturing, through the Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund (LSIMF). The scheme launched in October 2024 and will deliver funding until March 2030. Projects focused on Advanced Therapy ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, for a list of all grants made from the Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund to date, identifying the grant amount and the recipient company.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed up to £520m for life sciences manufacturing through the Life Sciences Innovative Manufacturing Fund (LSIMF). The scheme launched in October 2024 and will deliver funding until March 2030. To date the scheme has made nine grant...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescriptions: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many NHS prescriptions were issued to not ordinarily resident overseas visitors in (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This data is not held centrally.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many not ordinarily resident overseas visitors attended at NHS (a) walk in centres and (b) A&E departments in (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data is not available publicly in the format requested.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Tomography
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many (a) CT scanners and (b) MRI scanners were in use in the NHS at (i) 4 July 2024 and (ii) 14 May 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is no published data showing the number of computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanners in use on 4 July 2024 and 14 May 2026.Data on the number of CT and MRI scanners in use can be found in the Diagnostics Imaging dataset a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what (a) number and (b) proportion of chargeable cases of NHS secondary health care services supplied to not ordinarily resident overseas visitors saw full recovery of all charges due in (i) 2024-2
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department neither holds nor routinely collects data on the number of overseas visitors receiving National Health Service secondary care, or on the volume or proportion of charges recovered.The Department publishes data on the income identified from c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Foreign Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many not ordinarily resident overseas visitors received NHS secondary health care services, regardless of whether a charging expedition applied, in (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department neither holds nor routinely collects data on the number of overseas visitors receiving National Health Service secondary care, or on the volume or proportion of charges recovered.The Department publishes data on the income identified from c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Propriety and Constitution Group: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to his statement to the House on 23 February 2026 in Hansard volume 781, column 32, by what date does he expect to launch the external recruitment campaign for the role of Director General for Propriety
writtenQuestions.answerText: A recruitment campaign for the permanent appointment will be launched in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Nitrous Oxide: Misuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the misuse of nitrous oxide canisters sold for catering purposes on the environment; and if she will consider introducing a depo
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made of the potential impact of the misuse of nitrous oxide canisters on the environment and we are not considering a deposit return scheme for such canisters. It’s an offence to illegally dispose of nitrous oxide cannisters. Local ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Empty Property: Council Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment has been made of the impact of council tax liability on estates where the sale of a deceased person’s property is delayed beyond six months; and whether the Gover
writtenQuestions.answerText: When a property has been left empty following the death of its owner or occupant, it is exempt from council tax for as long as it remains unoccupied and until probate is granted. Following a grant of probate (or the signing of letters of administration), ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Empty Property: Council Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many applications for discretionary council tax reductions on empty properties following bereavement have been (a) made and (b) granted by local authorities in each of the pa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The administration of council tax is the responsibility of local authorities, this involves administering exemptions and discounts. The department does not hold data on applications for discretionary council tax reductions on empty properties following be...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what progress he has made on the rollout of the Infected Blood Compensation Scheme; how many individuals have (a) been assessed for compensation, (b) received offers and (c) received payments; what the average p
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Infected Blood Compensation Authority (IBCA) publishes updated statistics every fortnight. As of 2 June, 4,466 people have been contacted by IBCA to start their compensation claim, and 4,204 people have started the claim process. 3,378 offers of compe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the cost of extending equivalent business rates support provided to retail and hospitality to community pharmacies; and what assessment he has made of the potential imp
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the Autumn Budget 2025, the Government made hard choices so that we could protect the National Health Service in England and continue to prioritise reducing waiting times. Budget 2025 confirmed that annual NHS day-to-day spending will increase by £15 b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Free Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what her planned timeline is for the delivery of new free schools approved in principle, including the (a) key stages from approval to opening, (b) typical duration of each stage and (c) factors impacting deliv
writtenQuestions.answerText: New free school projects that are centrally delivered by the department typically progress through six key stages: pre‑feasibility (including finding a suitable site), feasibility, procurement, design and planning, construction and handover, and post‑comp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Incinerators: Cambridgeshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether the Health and Safety Executive will publish the DSEAR assessment for the proposed MVV facility in Cambridgeshire under the Environmental Information Regulations once completed.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Dangerous Substances and Explosive Atmospheres Regulations 2002 (DSEAR) require employers to assess the risks to safety from fire, explosions and substances corrosive to metals and put control measures in place to either remove the risks or, where thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Infrastructure: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether local planning authorities are required to (a) retain and (b) disclose DSEAR assessments for major infrastructure projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local planning authorities (LPAs) are not under a general statutory duty to either (a) retain or (b) disclose DSEAR assessments for major infrastructure projects. The legal duties under DSEAR sit primarily with the employer/operator (the duty holder). The...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Renewable Energy: Waste
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether statutory consultees are required to assess risks from (a) lithium-ion batteries and (b) gas canisters when reviewing fire prevention plans for energy-from-waste facilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Environment Agency is responsible for reviewing fire prevention plans (FPP) for energy-from-waste (EfW) facilities in England under the Environmental Permitting Regulations (EPR) 2016. Under the EPR, there are no statutory consultees. However, the Env...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Waiting Lists
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Answer of 29 April 2026 to Question 82312 on Hospitals: Waiting Lists, what were the monthly figures for October 2025 to April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Over 85% of removals from the Referral to Treatment (RTT) waiting list are recorded as completed pathways. This includes patients starting their first definitive treatment, starting a period of active monitoring, a clinical decision not to treat, or a pat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
